thesis: Adaptive viability is the geometry of recoverable future transformational accessibility.

R_plus.name: Reachable Future Accessibility Structure
R_plus.definition: Set of survivably reachable future states accessible from a recursively indexed state over horizon T under admissible dynamics.

V_plus.name: Reachable Future Accessibility Measure
V_plus.definition: Measure of survivably reachable future-state accessibility.

T_plus.name: Transformational Accessibility Structure
T_plus.definition: Defines what transformational futures are survivably reachable.

Omega_reopen.name: Transformational Reopenability Measure
Omega_reopen.definition: Defines which reachable transformations preserve recoverable continuity.

canonical_compressions: Adaptive systems persist by preserving survivably reachable futures.
Adaptive viability is the geometry of recoverable future transformational accessibility.
Persistence is preserved reopenable transformation geometry.
Collapse is loss of reopenable transformation geometry.
